ogTitle: Sandy Gully vs Sawyers Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sandy Gully and Sawyers Valley.
heading: Sandy Gully vs Sawyers Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sandy Gully, Western Australia and Sawyers Valley, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sawyers Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Sandy Gully (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sandy Gully or Sawyers Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sandy Gully has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Sawyers Valley scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sandy Gully scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Sawyers Valley scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sandy Gully or Sawyers Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sawyers Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
